regime: Holiday (Independence Day Observed), Cash Closed; Carried Risk-Off Day 3
summary: A holiday bridge, not a session. Friday July 3 was the observed Independence Day holiday (July 4 Saturday). Cash equity markets were closed and CME micro futures traded only a thin half-session to the ~1:00 pm ET early close, so there was no new cash RTH structure. A live data pull was not available in this unattended run, so all levels are carried forward from the Thu Jul 2 close and flagged as such. The Jul 2 risk-off Day 3 read stands into the long weekend: equities are both-short on the 1hr (MNQ 29,551.75 below a 29,783 falling stop, MES 7,522.75 below 7,538), while gold holds its safe-haven long (4,164.7 above 4,124) and crude holds long (68.47 above 67.43). No trade signal fired; the book is flat and the throttle DEFENSIVE. The single open question is a Monday gap after a three-day break on one soft jobs print (June NFP +57K). Standing map targets Monday Jul 6 and carries intact.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (FLAT, stopped out)
setups.entry: No position; the long is stopped out and the system is flat
setups.t1: Re-arm only on a 2-close reclaim of the 29,783 flipped stop, judged on the Jul 6 session
setups.bias: OUT_OF_HABITAT. No cash session on the holiday and no in-habitat trend. Do not chase or bottom-fish a thin, cash-closed tape or carry directional size over the three-day weekend. Wait for a live Jul 6 setup.

setups.name: Holiday / No Session (all edges out)
setups.entry: No cash RTH session Jul 3; all seven Net Alpha Pro core edges out-of-habitat
setups.t1: First live catalyst Mon Jul 6 ISM Services (Jun) 10:00 ET
setups.bias: Flat is the correct full position into the reopen. Treat Monday's open like an event open: the first OR break is suspect after a long weekend and a single jobs print.

event.name: No scheduled release (Independence Day observed; cash markets closed, futures early close)
event.actual: n/a (market holiday). The tape that still matters is Thursday's June jobs report: NFP +57K vs about +115K consensus, May revised to +129K, a downside miss.
event.surprise: None today. Monday's open is the first real chance to reprice the soft June jobs number after a three-day weekend.
event.nqReaction: No cash session. Carried: MNQ stands 29,551.75 (-1.71% Jul 2), below a falling short 29,783 stop, both-short with MES.
event.crossAsset: Carried from Jul 2: gold long (4,164.7, +2.26%, safe-haven bid holds above the 4,124 flipped stop), crude long (68.47, +0.57%), ES resilient and NQ-concentrated. Nothing today changed the alignment.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: Carried. The Monday post-weekend gap after a three-day break is the setup to watch, not a clean fade yet.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: Carried. The longer-horizon long is intact above its multi-month base but tested by the short-horizon flip short.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: Carried. MES both-short with NQ on the 1hr, keeping the damage tech-concentrated.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. The book is flat by design: TSMOM-20 is stopped out with a short, falling stop and, being long-only, takes no trade. On a holiday there is no in-habitat trend to size into.
risk.ddProximity: Not the binding issue with a flat book. The full daily budget is intact.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact. The binding risk is behavioral and gap-driven, not a gap to a stop.
risk.sizing: No new directional risk on a thin, cash-closed tape. Do not carry size over the three-day weekend; an overnight-and-weekend gap on a single jobs print is exactly what a flat book removes. The long-only equity stack takes neither the gold nor the crude long.
risk.note: Week ahead: Mon Jul 6 ISM Services (Jun) 10:00 ET (first catalyst after the break); Wed Jul 8 FOMC June-meeting minutes 14:00 ET.

psychology: A closed market is a gift, not a void. The temptation on a quiet holiday is to fill the silence, to scroll thin futures ticks and talk yourself into a position that does not exist yet. There is no edge in a cash-closed half-session, and no rule that says you must have a trade on before Monday. The professional move is to rest, let the June jobs number breathe over the long weekend, and arrive Monday with a clear head and a defined plan: the 29,783 stop overhead, the 29,500 / 29,328 shelf below, and a demand for a clean in-habitat setup before risking anything. Composure compounds. Monday will still be there.

Holiday bridge: the Jul 2 risk-off structure stands into the long weekend, equities both-short on the 1hr, gold and crude long. No cash session set new structure; the dominant axes today are Liquidity (thin) and Event (no releases). The one open question is a Monday gap after a three-day break on a single soft jobs print.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). No cash session today, so there is no opening range or COMPASS structure to grade. Standing reference from Jul 2: OR 30,007 to 30,188 broke UP and FAILED into a trend-down day. On Monday, treat the post-holiday open like an event open, the first OR break is suspect after a three-day weekend and a single jobs print.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). Long invalid, carried. Price sits below AVWAP and below a short, falling 1hr stop; the regime filter is negative. Fresh long signals require a 2-close reclaim of the 29,783 stop, not a first touch, and that can only be judged on the Jul 6 session. The E3v2 momentum-long edge stays out-of-habitat.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Carried. Center 29,581, sigma 180. Bands: +1s 29,760, +2s 29,940, +3s 30,119; -1s 29,401, -2s 29,221, -3s 29,042. Price stands 29,551.75, just below AVWAP in the -1s zone, not stretched to a reversion trigger. A long-side fade re-qualifies only on a -2s / -3s tag with an exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size, and only on a live session. Bands are price-based (volume unavailable; Alphatrends indicator not loaded).

fullReport.title: Strategy Habitat Check
fullReport.html: Strategy Habitat Check. No strategy is in-habitat on a holiday with no cash session. All seven Net Alpha Pro core edges (TSMOM-20, AVWAP Deviation Band, AVWAP Bounce, AVWAP Hand-off, AVWAP Midrange, AVWAP Back Into Range, E3v2 Momentum Long) are OUT_OF_HABITAT. TSMOM-20 1hr (long-only) is flat (stopped out, stop short and falling) and takes no trade; there is no session to run the AVWAP reversion, COMPASS session, or E3v2 momentum edges. Flat is the correct full position into the reopen.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (status of the standing map)
fullReport.html:
The Jul 2 report set its map for Monday, July 6, deliberately skipping the closed Friday. Today was the observed holiday with no cash session, so there is nothing to grade against: these rows remain PENDING and carry to the Jul 6 EOD run. No row is dropped or re-graded, and PENDING is a neutral state, not a miss.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Monday, July 6, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Monday, July 6, 2026 (levels carried from the Thu Jul 2 close; no session Jul 3). Filters Monday: Monday gates out BIR (skip Mon); HOF, MID, and BNC are eligible by weekday. The HOF dead zone 12:00 to 14:00 ET still applies, and MID only in the 9:30 to 10:30 ET window. Treat the post-holiday open as a gap-risk open after a three-day weekend and a single jobs print. Calendar: Mon Jul 6 ISM Services (Jun) 10:00 ET; Wed Jul 8 FOMC June-meeting minutes 14:00 ET.
